description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In `core.py` line 317, guard with `char_count = len(result.stdout or "")` or ensure the subprocess call passes `stdout=subprocess.PIPE`.
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. Root cause: `collect_hook_output_size` calls `len(result.stdout)` but `result.stdout` is `None` because `subprocess.run` was called without `capture_output=True` or `stdout=PIPE`, so stdout was not captured.. Diagnosis log: memory\learning\failures\2026-04-16_self-diagnose-jarvis-heartbeat-2.md

description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In collectors/core.py run_collector (or the subprocess invocation feeding collect_hook_output_size), decode subprocess output with encoding="utf-8" and errors="replace", and guard char_count = len(result.stdout or "").
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. isc: jarvis_heartbeat exits cleanly after fix | Verify: python tools/scripts/jarvis_heartbeat.py --test

description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In `collectors/core.py` run the subprocess with `encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"` (or capture bytes) and guard `result.stdout` with `result.stdout or ""` before calling `len()`.
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. Root cause: A subprocess invoked by `collect_hook_output_size` emitted non-cp1252 bytes (0x90) on stdout, causing the reader thread to raise UnicodeDecodeError; `result.stdout` was then None, and `len(result.stdout)` raised TypeError.. description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In tools/scripts/collectors/core.py:312, pass encoding="utf-8", errors="replace" to subprocess.run (and guard `len(result.stdout or "")`) so Unicode output from hooks decodes correctly on Windows.
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. Root cause: collect_hook_output_size ran a hook subprocess with text=True on Windows (cp1252 default); the hook emitted bytes (0x90) that cp1252 cannot decode, the reader thread raised UnicodeDecodeError, result.stdout was set to None, and len(None) crashed the collector.. Diagnosis log: memory\learning\failures\2026-04-16_self-diagnose-jarvis-heartbeat-7.md
isc: jarvis_heartbeat exits cleanly after fix | Verify: python tools/scripts/jarvis_heartbeat.py --test
notes: Auto-generated by self-diagnose-wrapper L1. Category: code_bug core.py fix committed 2e4d14b (2026-04-17); superseded. [closed]

description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In collectors/core.py collect_hook_output_size, run the subprocess with encoding='utf-8' and errors='replace' (or capture bytes and decode defensively), and guard against result.stdout being None before calling len().
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. Root cause: A subprocess invoked by collect_hook_output_size produced non-cp1252 bytes (0x90) on stdout, causing Python's default cp1252 decoder to raise UnicodeDecodeError in the reader thread, which left result.stdout as None and then triggered TypeError when len() was called on it.. Diagnosis log: memory\learning\failures\2026-04-16_self-diagnose-jarvis-heartbeat-8.md
isc: jarvis_heartbeat exits cleanly after fix | Verify: python tools/scripts/jarvis_heartbeat.py --test

description: Run /synthesize-signals to distill accumulated learning signals into a synthesis doc
goal_context: Learning compounds -- threshold model is 10 signals / 72h. This routine fires every 3 days so the worst case is one missed cycle. If no signals are queued, /synthesize-signals exits Idle Is Success.
isc: A new synthesis document exists in memory/learning/synthesis/ dated today, OR worker reports Idle Is Success when no unprocessed signals queued | Verify: python tools/scripts/verify_synthesis_routine.py

notes: Routine-injected by routines engine. Switched 2026-04-08: skill learning-capture -> synthesize-signals (skill mismatch -- learning-capture is for new signals, synthesize-signals is for distillation). interval 7 -> 3 to match the 72h tier in hook_session_start. routine_id kept stable for state continuity. Tier 1 + expected_outputs declares the write surface for scope_creep.

isc: acquire_claude_lock() returns False or similar skip signal when lock is held, without killing the dispatcher process | Verify: grep -c sys.exit tools/scripts/jarvis_dispatcher.py

notes: Queued 2026-04-19 backlog triage. Root cause: acquire_claude_lock() exits the entire dispatcher process when overnight lock is held, should skip+continue instead.
